title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Transfer Build
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers complexity, profiling, rendering budgets, and responsiveness into a changed representative performance and responsiveness report. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of representative performance and responsiveness report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Complexity Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 6639 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Capacity Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-1iys9q" that uses case 3101 to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Capacity Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capacity Defense into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Capacity Defense, keep Capacity Defense connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-capacity-defense-1iys9q
# Competency: maze-capacity-defense.
# Case 3101: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_capacity_defense_1iys9q(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1iys9q = "1iys9q-q9syi1-3101"
    assert evidence_variant_1iys9q
    evidence_axes_1iys9q = (
        "grid1iys9q",
        "topology3101",
        "searchq9syi1",
        "event1iys9q3101",
        "access31011iys9q",
        "recoveryq9syi13101",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1iys9q)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"
